questionText: Prove that the difference between any two odd numbers is even.

steps.explanation: Every odd number has the form $2a+1$.
The second odd number is independent, so it uses its own letter $b$.
The statement is about subtracting one odd number from another.
Distributing the minus sign changes the sign of both terms in the second bracket.
The $+1$ and $-1$ cancel, leaving only the even terms.
Pulling out $2$ shows the difference is twice an integer.
Since $a-b$ is an integer, the difference is $2\times(\text{integer})$, hence even; the difference of two odd numbers is always even.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $(2a+1)-(2b+1)=2a-2b=2(a-b)$, which is even. Hence the difference of two odd numbers is even. $\blacksquare$
